sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 32000078
between the City and DC Electric Group, Inc. for supplemental On-Call electrical
services to support the Public Works Electrical Unit, increasing the current contract
spending authority by $250,000 for a new total not to exceed amount of $725,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
This is an On-Call Contract; costs are incurred when services are performed. Initial
funding is available in FY25 Fund 134 and FY25 Fund 142. Additional funding will be
appropriated as part of the FY25 Second Annual Appropriations Ordinance and in future
fiscal years as needed.
sections.current_situation:
The Public Works’ Electrical Unit supports the City’s electrical needs by installing,
repairing and replacing electrical wiring and components in City facilities and maintains
City assets which rely on electric energy to operate; assets such as Traffic Signals,
Pedestrian Crossing Signals, Street Lights, Marina Pier Lights, and Park Lights. Public
Works’ Electrical Unit has been utilizing the on-call services of DC Electric Group to
help resolve a back log of damaged Traffic Signals and Street Lights as well as to
support the Unit with ongoing need for the repair of Traffic Signals and Street Lights
damaged by vehicles. Increased contract spending authority is needed to be able to
continue repairing damaged assets while also performing preventive maintenance on
working assets to ensure community safety.
Amending the DC Electric Contract supports the City’s Strategic Plan Priority of
providing state-of-the art, well maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
On November 3, 2016, Public Works issued a Request for Proposals for On-Call
Electrical Services to support the Public Works Facilities Electrical Unit (Specification
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 3
Contract No. 32000078 Amendment DC Electric CONSENT CALENDAR
Group, Inc for On-Call Electrical Service December 10, 2024
No. 16-11076-C.) Four proposals were submitted and two vendors were selected: DC
Electric Group and Colombia Electric. On the authority of Resolution Number 68,007-
N.S., the City contracted with DC Electric Group on June 1, 2017 and continues to
utilize their on-call services to supplement the work of the City’s Electrical Unit.
On July 26, 2022, City Council approved an amendment for a revised not to exceed
amount of $175,000 and to extend the contract through June 30, 2025.
On September 19, 2023, City Council approved an amendment for a revised not to
exceed amount of $475,000 and to extend the contract through June 30, 2026.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identified environmental or climate impacts associated with the subject of
this report.
sections.rationale:
DC Electric Group continues to on-call services for the City to support the City’s in-
house Electrical Unit in maintaining the City’s critical electrical assets and infrastructure.
Without DC’s services, needed maintenance and repairs would face delays.
Public Works is planning to release a solicitation for Electric Services in 2025, so this
amendment gives the department time to complete the RFP process without causing
delays or interruptions in service. In addition, the Electrical Unit is in the process of
extending offers to hire two electricians to backfill long term vacancies.
sections.alternatives:
None. Public Works Electrical Unit does not have adequate resources to keep up with
the ongoing maintenance and repairs of deployed electrical assets. It also does not
have the capacity to provide services to install electrical wiring and components in new
or renovated City facilities. Therefore, no alternative actions were considered.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Operations Manager, Public Works, (510) 981-6629
Aaron Baker, Facilities Superintendent, Public Works, (510) 981-6452
Peni Basalusalu, Deputy Director, Public Works, (510) 981-6485
Tucker Brofft, Senior Management Analyst, Public Works, (510) 981-5375
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32000078 AMENDMENT: DC ELECTRICAL GROUP FOR ON-CALL
ELECTRICAL SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City requires supplemental emergency and non-emergency on-call
electrical services for the repair, maintenance and replacement of multiple types of
electrical assets and for the maintenance and installation of electrical infrastructure in City
facilities; and
WHEREAS, the City does not have sufficient resources to provide the required
maintenance and repair of the City’s electrical assets in a timely or cost-effective manner;
and
WHEREAS, the City issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) Specification No. 16-11076-C
for on-call electrical services on November 3, 2016, four contractors responded to the
RFP on December 8. 2016; two contractors met all requirements and conditions of the
RFP; and
WHEREAS, staff reviewed and evaluated all proposals submitted and on authority of
Council Resolution No. 68,007-N.S., contracted with DC Electric Group on June 1, 2016;
and
WHEREAS, on July 26, 2022, City Council authorized the City Manager to execute a
Contract Amendment to increase the Contract’s spending authority by $100,000 for a new
not to exceed amount of $175,000 and to extend the Contract through June 30, 2025;
and
WHEREAS, on September 19, 2023, City Council authorized the City Manager to execute
a Contract Amendment to increase the Contract’s spending authority by $300,000 for a
new not to exceed amount of $475,000 and to extend the Contract through June 30, 2026;
and
WHEREAS, this Contract is for on-call services only; no costs will be incurred until
applicable City projects or emergency needs are identified and Contractor services are
requested; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the Street Light Assessment District Fund 142 and
Measure BB – Local Streets and Road Fund 134 and additional funding will be
appropriated as part of the FY25 Second Annual Appropriations Ordinance and in future
fiscal years as needed.
N
